goal: Lean pipeline redesign — surgical, incremental fixes to reduce context bloat and resolve conflicts without breaking existing features

risks: Conflict resolution requires design decisions, not just editorial fixes — project-brainstorm must specify resolution direction for each conflict
09-execute-projects.md wave algorithm is ~900 words — must decide whether to keep inline, compress, or extract before setting trim target
No verification that trimmed docs still work — need smoke-test DOD
over_engineering_flags: First attempt created 7 new files — this plan creates zero new pipeline docs
Enforcement goes into existing validation scripts, not more prose
challenges.recommendation: Proceed. 3 projects, sequential by dependency. Word target reframed as ceiling (under 4000) per CTO+CPO review.

projects.title: Resolve 7 documented pipeline doc conflicts in-place
projects.scope_summary: Fix all 7 conflicts identified in the brainstorm by editing existing pipeline docs in-place. Each fix is a small edit — no new files, no restructuring. Resolution direction for each conflict must be specified in project-brainstorm.

projects.title: Trim 09-execute-projects.md to under 4000 words
projects.scope_summary: Trim the single largest pipeline doc in-place. Remove redundant restatements, consolidate duplicate blocks, replace verbose prose with bullets. Promote buried project.json update instruction to prominent callout. Wave algorithm: compress inline, do not extract. Target: under 4000 words preserving all critical enforcement content. Add smoke-test DOD.

projects.title: Add 3 critical missing enforcement gates as validation scripts
projects.scope_summary: Implement 3 highest-impact enforcement gates as mechanical validation (scripts), not prose. Gate 1: project.json update verification. Gate 2: browser test verification. Gate 3: code-review failure blocking. All changes in existing files — no new scripts.
